# SRC002 - How Well Does Advertising Work? Generalizations from Meta-Analysis of Brand Advertising Elasticities

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | Sethuraman, Tellis & Briesch |
| Year | 2011 |
| Source type | Meta-analysis |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://gwern.net/doc/economics/advertising/2011-sethuraman.pdf](https://gwern.net/doc/economics/advertising/2011-sethuraman.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic meta-analysis; brand-level elasticities, not SaaS-specific.

## Core takeaway

Advertising effects are real but often modest; elasticity varies by context.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use as a dose of realism: campaigns need enough repetition, budget, fit, and measurement; one ad is not magic.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC002

- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
